Notice Of Motion For Order To Bring Witness Ricky Mccloud Before Court To Testify At Trial, Or In The Altenrative, To Compel Mccloud To Answer Questions At Deposition, With Request For Sanctions Agains Tpltf'S Counsel;
Off Calendar Oct-09-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant 11th & Folsom Community Centers' motion for an order to bring a witness to testify at trial or to compel responses to questions at deposition is granted in part. Pursuant to CCP Section 187 and 1995, it is hereby ordered that the Sheriff for the County of Solano permit the release of Ricky Jay McCloud Jr., a person currently incarcerated in the Solano County Jail, to the custody of the Sheriff for the City and County of San Francisco for transportation to the trial of the above-entitled action. The Sheriff for the City and County of San Francisco shall transport the prisoner on a date to be determined by the Court. Any and all costs associated with the transportation, housing, and supervision of the prisoner shall be borne by defendant and paid prior to his transfer. Upon completion of his testimony, or pursuant to court order, the prisoner shall be returned forthwith to the custody of the Sheriff for the County of Solano.

By ordering the transportation of the witness, the Court makes no ruling as to the propriety of his testimony and any objection to said testimony by plaintiff pursuant to Evidence Code Section 352 or any other provision of law. Furthermore, the Court makes no ruling as to the assertion of any privilege by the witness.

All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Ntc Of Mtn To Re-Open Discovery For The Limited Purpose Of 1)Permitting Deft'S Medical Expert To Examine Plntf 2)Allowing Defts To Depose Witness Ricky Mccloud
Off Calendar Aug-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Susan B. Cohen, Esq., for defendant and moving party: John Stevens, Zeremy Uptegrove and 11th & Folsom Preservation and Community Centers, Inc.
Joseph J. Poppen, Esq., for plaintiff and mopposing party: Aaron Anderson

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Stevens' motion to re-open discovery to conduct an independent medical examination and a deposition is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to permitting an examination of plaintiff. The Court finds that defendant was not diligent in seeking to examine plaintiff. The motion is granted as to the deposition of Ricky McCloud. The deposition shall be completed by September 15, 2007 at a date mutually convenient to the parties. If the deposition is videotaped, the videographer shall ensure that the location of the deposition is not apparent from the film. In addition, the appearance of the witness shall not indicate that he is in custody. The request for monetary sanctions by plaintiff is denied. Order signed in open court. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

MINI MINUTES:
